name: CRUD/conditional-update
description: Conditional update (PUT /Patient?search-params) conformance tests: upsert-on-no-match, 400 on no criteria, body-id agreement/mismatch handling, and 412 on multiple matches.

variable.name: runId
variable.description: Runner-generated per-suite value used to isolate fixed-id create tests

variable.name: cuNoMatchId
variable.description: Server-assigned id found after the CU-NOMATCH conditional update

variable.name: seedOneMatchId
variable.description: Server-assigned id found for the Patient created in setup that carries identifier CU-ONEMATCH

operation.description: Unconditionally create the Patient that later conditional-update attempts should match exactly once (CU-ONEMATCH)

assert.description: Setup create for CU-ONEMATCH Patient must return 201 Created

operation.description: Find the current-run CU-ONEMATCH Patient without depending on an optional create response body

assert.description: Setup search must return exactly one CU-ONEMATCH Patient entry

operation.description: PUT a client-id Patient (CU-CORRECTID) so later tests can deterministically match or mismatch its id in a conditional update body

assert.description: Setup PUT Patient/ignixa-cond-upd-correctid (CU-CORRECTID) must succeed

test.name: Conditional update with no existing match upserts a new resource
test.description: PUT /Patient?identifier=... matching no existing resource with an id-less body; the server must upsert (create) it and return 201 Created.

operation.description: Conditional PUT for the current-run CU-NOMATCH identifier, matching nothing

assert.description: Must return HTTP 201 Created

operation.description: Find the current-run created Patient because a conditional update response body is optional

assert.description: Search must return exactly one created Patient entry
Server must have assigned an id

test.name: Conditional update with no search criteria is rejected
test.description: PUT /Patient with no search criteria; the server must reject the request with 400 Bad Request.

operation.description: Conditional PUT /Patient with no query string

assert.description: Must return HTTP 400 Bad Request

test.name: Conditional update with a client-supplied id and no existing match creates that id
test.description: PUT /Patient?identifier=... matching no existing resource with a body carrying a client-supplied id; the server must create the resource using that id and return 201 Created.

operation.description: Conditional PUT /Patient?identifier=...CU-EXPLICIT-${runId}, body carries id=ignixa-cond-upd-explicit-${runId}

assert.description: Must return HTTP 201 Created

operation.description: Read back the created Patient because a conditional update response body is optional

assert.description: Created Patient must be readable at the client-supplied id
Server must have used the client-supplied id

test.name: Conditional update with no id in body and one match updates the existing resource
test.description: PUT /Patient?identifier=... matching exactly one existing Patient with an id-less body; the server must update it in place and return 200 OK with the same id.

operation.description: Conditional PUT matching the single current-run CU-ONEMATCH Patient

assert.description: Must return HTTP 200 OK (updated, not created)

operation.description: Read back the matched Patient because a conditional update response body is optional

assert.description: Updated Patient must be readable
Updated id must match the existing seeded resource's id

test.name: Conditional update with a body id matching the resolved resource updates its content
test.description: PUT /Patient?identifier=... matching one existing Patient whose id agrees with the body's id; the server must accept the update and return 200 OK with the updated content.

operation.description: Conditional PUT /Patient?identifier=...CU-CORRECTID, body id matches the resolved resource's id

assert.description: Must return HTTP 200 OK

operation.description: Read back the matched Patient because a conditional update response body is optional

assert.description: Updated Patient must be readable
Id must remain ignixa-cond-upd-correctid
Content must reflect the update (gender changed to female)

test.name: Conditional update with a mismatched body id is rejected
test.description: PUT /Patient?identifier=... matching one existing Patient whose id disagrees with the body's id; the server must reject the request with 400 Bad Request.

operation.description: Conditional PUT /Patient?identifier=...CU-CORRECTID, body id deliberately disagrees with the resolved resource's id

assert.description: Must return HTTP 400 Bad Request

test.name: Conditional update with multiple existing matches fails
test.description: PUT /Patient?identifier=... matching two existing Patients; the server must return 412 Precondition Failed since the criteria isn't selective enough.

operation.description: PUT the first current-run Patient sharing the CU-MULTI identifier

assert.description: First CU-MULTI setup PUT must return 201 Created

operation.description: PUT the second current-run Patient sharing the CU-MULTI identifier

assert.description: Second CU-MULTI setup PUT must return 201 Created

operation.description: Conditional PUT matching both current-run CU-MULTI Patients

assert.description: Must return HTTP 412 Precondition Failed

operation.description: Teardown: delete the current-run CU-NOMATCH Patient by server-assigned id
Teardown: delete the current-run explicit-id conditional-update Patient ignixa-cond-upd-explicit-${runId}
Teardown: delete the current-run CU-ONEMATCH Patient by server-assigned id
Teardown: delete the current-run CU-CORRECTID Patient by fixed id
Teardown: delete the first current-run CU-MULTI Patient by client id
Teardown: delete the second current-run CU-MULTI Patient by client id
Teardown: defensively delete the mismatched-body id if a nonconforming server wrote it
Teardown fallback: conditionally delete current-run CU-NOMATCH leftovers
Teardown fallback: conditionally delete CU-EXPLICIT-${runId} leftovers or contamination
Teardown fallback: conditionally delete current-run CU-ONEMATCH leftovers
Teardown fallback: conditionally delete CU-CORRECTID leftovers or contamination
Teardown fallback: conditionally delete current-run CU-MULTI leftovers
